title: Walking Meditation
blurb: Mindfulness in motion — for when sitting still is hard.
lead: Walking meditation brings mindful attention to the simple act of walking. It's ideal when sitting still feels difficult or restless.

blocks.text: Walking meditation means walking slowly and deliberately, with full attention on the movement and sensations of each step.
It's a wonderful option for restless minds, and it weaves mindfulness into everyday movement.

items: Choose a quiet stretch where you can walk slowly back and forth, or a calm path.
Stand still and feel your feet on the ground.
Begin walking slowly, noticing the lifting, moving, and placing of each foot.
When the mind wanders, return attention to the sensations of walking.
Keep your gaze soft and pace unhurried.

items.label: Best for
items.value: Restlessness, anxiety, integrating mindfulness into daily life

blocks.text: Walking too fast out of habit. Slower than normal helps attention stay with the movement.

resources.title: Medito
resources.whyHere: Free non-profit app with movement-based practices.
